message:
feat(obligation): add typestate linear obligation tokens with drop-bomb enforcement

Introduce a sealed-trait typestate system for obligation lifecycle tracking
that approximates linear types in Rust's affine type system. Tokens created
via reserve() must be explicitly resolved via commit() or abort() — dropping
an unresolved token panics with "OBLIGATION TOKEN LEAKED", catching resource
leaks at runtime during testing.

- Add ObligationToken<K> with PermitReserved/PermitCommitted/PermitAborted states
- Add four marker kinds: SendPermit, AckKind, LeaseKind, IoOpKind via sealed TokenKind trait
- Add CommittedProof<K>/AbortedProof<K> zero-sized witness types with bridge to ResolvedProof
- Add GradedScope integration: reserve_token(), resolve_commit(), resolve_abort()
- Add into_raw() escape hatch for FFI/migration scenarios
- Add type aliases: SendPermitToken, AckToken, LeaseToken, IoOpToken
- Include 11 tests covering commit/abort proofs, drop bomb, raw escape, proof bridging,
  kind mapping, scope integration, and all four token variants

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
